key_points: The poem explores the resilience of trees and the deliberate effort required to destroy them.
It uses vivid imagery and personification to portray the tree as a living, enduring entity.
The poem highlights human cruelty and environmental destruction through metaphorical language.
Themes include environmental awareness, persistence of life, and moral responsibility toward nature.
The poet contrasts superficial cutting of branches with the deeper roots, symbolizing strength and life force.
examples: The bark and trunk are described as layers that resist human attack, symbolizing endurance.
Personification: the tree ‘bleeds’ and suffers, showing life as more than physical structure.
The roots’ continued life represents the deep, hidden strength of nature and resilience against human harm.
exam_tips: Focus on literary devices: imagery, personification, metaphor, and symbolism.
Analyze how the poet portrays the tree’s life and endurance.
Understand the deeper message about human responsibility and environmental awareness.
Pay attention to the stages of killing a tree as symbolic of deliberate harm versus natural resilience.
Be able to discuss the poem’s tone: cautionary, serious, and reflective.
